success_factors: Workflow status is success with all expected nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy is appropriate for NetworkX and backed by high import feasibility (0.97).
Generated service started healthy over stdio and exposed a broad, meaningful endpoint set (34 endpoints).
Low intrusiveness risk and medium complexity repository profile reduced integration friction.
No runtime errors or warnings were reported during workflow execution.
failure_reasons: Validation gap: original project test status is marked not passed/empty despite reported 100% coverage metadata, indicating test instrumentation inconsistency.
Observability gap: node-level timings and resource metrics are missing, limiting precise performance diagnosis.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing depth of automated architectural context.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully as part of total 225.9s; repository imported via zip fallback (740 files), which is generally slower than native clone but acceptable.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully; structural/dependency/risk analysis produced coherent module map and endpoint candidates.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; 7 plugin artifacts generated with import-mode architecture and functional endpoint inventory.
node_performance.test_time: Plugin runtime health passed; however, endpoint-level functional tests were not executed or not recorded, so confidence is operational rather than behavioral.

resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate inferred footprint (few generated files), but generated LOC/size fields are zero, so exact usage is not measurable.
